note: HAL provides a serialization format plus normalizer/encoder services and a link manager, but no admin UI, no plugin type, and no permissions. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) since they probe documented facts (the format string, the core-to-contrib move, the link manager, custom normalizers, the serialization dependency) an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with the HAL module enabled, what is the serialization format string I pass to the core serializer to encode an entity as Hypertext Application Language, and which core service do I call?
evals.expected_output: Call the core `serializer` service's serialize()/normalize() with the format id `hal_json` (e.g. \Drupal::service('serializer')->serialize($entity, 'hal_json')).

evals.prompt: My Drupal 9 site used the core HAL module for hal_json output. After upgrading to Drupal 10/11, hal_json no longer works. What happened and how do I restore it?
evals.expected_output: HAL was removed from Drupal core in D10 and moved to the drupal/hal contrib module; require drupal/hal and enable it (drush en hal) to restore the hal_json format.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal site using HAL, which service builds the type and relation URIs that appear in an entity's _links, and which methods generate a bundle/type URI versus a field relation URI?
evals.expected_output: The hal.link_manager service (LinkManager, implementing LinkManagerInterface); getTypeUri() builds the bundle/type URI and getRelationUri() builds a field's relation URI. getTypeInternalIds()/getRelationInternalIds() resolve URIs back to internal IDs.

evals.prompt: I want to add a custom normalizer for a bespoke field type so it serializes correctly in HAL. What base class do I extend and how do I register the service so it applies to the hal_json format?
evals.expected_output: Extend Drupal\hal\Normalizer\NormalizerBase (its $format is hal_json) and register the service tagged { name: normalizer } with a priority high enough to beat the generic normalizer.

evals.prompt: Which core Drupal module does the contrib HAL module depend on, and what other module is typically enabled with it to expose hal_json over HTTP?
evals.expected_output: HAL depends on the core serialization module (its normalizers/encoder extend serialization's); it is typically paired with the rest module (and optionally Views REST exports) to serve hal_json over HTTP.

evals.prompt: The link URIs in my HAL output use the wrong host. Which config object and key set the domain used for HAL type/relation links, and what is the default behavior?
evals.expected_output: Set hal.settings:link_domain (e.g. drush cset hal.settings link_domain 'https://api.example.com'); when blank/null it defaults to the site's own domain. Programmatically, LinkManager::setLinkDomain() does the same.

evals.prompt: In a custom Drupal module I need to rewrite the type and relation URIs HAL generates for entities. Which hooks do I implement?
evals.expected_output: Implement hook_hal_type_uri_alter(&$uri, $context) for the type URI and hook_hal_relation_uri_alter(&$uri, $context) for relation URIs; both receive the URI by reference and the serializer context.
